bill.short_title: Continuing Appropriations Act, 2020, and Health Extenders Act of 2019

provisions.rate: at a rate for operations as provided in the applicable appropriations Acts for fiscal year 2019
provisions.raw_text: Such amounts as may be necessary, at a rate for operations as provided in the applicable appropriations Acts for fiscal year 2019 and under the authori
notes: Core CR mechanism funding all accounts at FY2019 rates through November 21, 2019
References 12 FY2019 appropriations acts across three public laws

(1) Agriculture (Div B of P.L. 116-6); (2) CJS (Div C of P.L. 116-6); (3) Defense (Div A of P.L. 115-245); (4) Energy-Water (Div A of P.L. 115-244); (5) FSGG (Div D of P.L. 116-6); (6) DHS (Div A of P.L. 116-6 as amended + Title I Div H of P.L. 116-6); (7) Interior (Div E of P.L. 116-6); (8) Labor-HHS (Div B of P.L. 115-245); (9) Leg Branch (Div B of P.L. 115-244); (10) MilCon-VA (Div C of P.L. 115-244); (11) State-FO (Div F of P.L. 116-6); (12) THUD (Div G of P.L. 116-6)

cross_references.description: FY2019: Agriculture (B), CJS (C), FSGG (D), Interior (E), State-FO (F), THUD (G), DHS (A + Title I of H)
FY2019: Defense (A), Labor-HHS (B)
FY2019: Energy-Water (A), Legislative Branch (B), MilCon-VA (C)

provisions.description: Prohibits DoD appropriations under the CR from being used for new production of items not funded in FY2019, increased production rates above FY2019 levels, or initiation/resumption of any project, activity, or organization not available in FY2019. Also prohibits initiating multi-year procurements utilizing advance procurement funding for economic order quantity unless specifically appropriated later.
provisions.raw_text:
(a) No appropriation or funds made available or authority granted pursuant to section 101 for the Department of Defense shall be used for: 
(1) the new
notes: Subsection (a) covers three categories: new production, increased rates, and new projects/activities/organizations
Subsection (b) separately prohibits multi-year procurements with advance procurement funding

provisions.description: Appropriations made by section 101 shall be available to the extent and in the manner that would be provided by the pertinent appropriations Act.
provisions.raw_text: Appropriations made by section 101 shall be available to the extent and in the manner that would be provided by the pertinent appropriations Act.
notes: Standard CR boilerplate ensuring same terms and conditions as underlying FY2019 acts

provisions.description: Prohibits use of CR funds to initiate or resume any project or activity for which appropriations, funds, or other authority were not available during fiscal year 2019, except as provided in section 102.
provisions.raw_text: Except as otherwise provided in section 102, no appropriation or funds made available or authority granted pursuant to section 101 shall be used to ini
notes: General prohibition on new starts under the CR
Section 102 provides the DoD-specific version of this limitation

cross_references.description: DoD-specific limitations take precedence

provisions.description: CR appropriations and authority shall cover all obligations or expenditures incurred for any project or activity during the period for which funds or authority are available under this Act.
provisions.raw_text: Appropriations made and authority granted pursuant to this Act shall cover all obligations or expenditures incurred for any project or activity during
notes: Standard CR boilerplate

provisions.description: Sets CR duration: funds available until enactment of specific appropriation, enactment of the applicable full-year FY2020 act without provision for such project or activity, or November 21, 2019, whichever occurs first.
provisions.raw_text: Unless otherwise provided for in this Act or in the applicable appropriations Act for fiscal year 2020, appropriations and funds made available and aut
notes: CR expiration date is November 21, 2019 (section 106(3))
Three termination triggers: (1) specific appropriation enacted, (2) full-year act without provision, (3) date reached

provisions.description: Expenditures made pursuant to this Act shall be charged to the applicable appropriation, fund, or authorization when the full-year bill is enacted into law.
provisions.raw_text: Expenditures made pursuant to this Act shall be charged to the applicable appropriation, fund, or authorization whenever a bill in which such applicabl
notes: Standard CR accounting treatment provision

provisions.description: CR funds may be used without regard to apportionment time limitations in 31 U.S.C. 1513, but does not waive other apportionment provisions.
provisions.raw_text: Appropriations made and funds made available by or authority granted pursuant to this Act may be used without regard to the time limitations for submiss
notes: Waives apportionment submission and approval deadlines only

cross_references.description: Apportionment time limitations

provisions.description: Programs with high initial rates of operation or complete distribution of appropriations at the beginning of FY2020 (distributions to states, foreign countries, grantees, or others) shall not make such distributions, and no grants shall be awarded that would impinge on final funding prerogatives.
provisions.raw_text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this Act, except section 106, for those programs that would otherwise have high initial rates of operation or co
notes: Prevents front-loading of CR funds for formula grants and similar programs
Exception for section 106 (duration provision)

provisions.description: The Act shall be implemented so that only the most limited funding action permitted shall be taken in order to provide for continuation of projects and activities.
provisions.raw_text: This Act shall be implemented so that only the most limited funding action of that permitted in the Act shall be taken in order to provide for continua
notes: Standard CR directive to minimize spending during the CR period

provisions.description: Entitlements and other mandatory payments with budget authority from FY2019 appropriations acts, and Food and Nutrition Act of 2008 activities, shall continue at rates to maintain current law program levels through November 21, 2019. Obligations for mandatory payments due on or about the first day of any month after October 2019 but within 30 days of November 21, 2019 may continue.
provisions.raw_text: (a) For entitlements and other mandatory payments whose budget authority was provided in appropriations Acts for fiscal year 2019, and for activities u
notes: Subsection (a): mandatory payments continue at current law program levels through Nov 21, 2019
Subsection (b): allows monthly payment obligations due after October 2019 but within 30 days of Nov 21, 2019
Includes SNAP (Food and Nutrition Act of 2008) activities

cross_references.description: SNAP benefits continuation

provisions.description: Civilian personnel compensation and benefits in each department and agency may be apportioned up to the rate necessary to avoid furloughs, consistent with FY2019 appropriations, but only after reducing or deferring non-personnel-related administrative expenses.

notes: Paragraph (1) reduces the current fund level from $1,000,000 to $0
Paragraph (3)(A) strikes '2023' and inserts '2025' each place it appears, extending the fund through FY2025
Paragraph (3)(A) also strikes '$0' and inserts '$2,387,000,000', establishing new mandatory spending
Net effect: zeros out the current $1M balance and creates $2,387,000,000 for FY2025, commonly used as a budget offset vehicle

flagged_issues: SEC. 124(a) rescinds unspecified unobligated balances from P.L. 115-245 section 9013 (Ukraine Security Assistance Initiative) — dollar amount unknown
SEC. 124(b) appropriates an amount equal to the rescission in SEC. 124(a) but dollar amount is not explicitly stated — excluded from total_budget_authority
SEC. 127 CFIUS Fund has $15M gross appropriation but estimated net general fund cost of not more than $5M after fee offsets
SEC. 129 OPM has $48M total but $29.76M financed from trust funds; net general fund cost is approximately $18.24M
total_budget_authority ($374,660,500) excludes the unquantifiable CR baseline from SEC. 101 and the unspecified amount in SEC. 124(b)
total_rescissions is null because SEC. 124(a) rescinds an unspecified amount of unobligated balances
All amounts in Title I are prorated mandatory spending extensions covering Oct 1 – Nov 21, 2019 (approximately 52 days of FY2020), consistent with the CR duration in Division A.
SEC. 1101(e) is a conforming amendment to 18 U.S.C. 3014(h)(4) — classified as 'other' since it is a technical cross-reference update, not a spending or policy provision.
Both SEC. 1201 and SEC. 1202 appropriate $10,684,931 each for a 52-day partial-year period (Oct 1 – Nov 21, 2019), which is a prorated amount consistent with the CR duration
All three provisions in Title III are Medicaid extenders with no explicit dollar amounts; they amend existing statutory dates and percentages
SEC. 1302 sets territorial FMAP to 100% for a short period, resulting in increased federal Medicaid spending for territories
SEC. 1303 delays DSH cuts, effectively providing short-term budget relief to states
All provisions in Title IV are mandatory spending extensions amending existing authorizing statutes, not discretionary appropriations
All dollar amounts represent pro-rated funding for the partial-year period October 1 through November 21, 2019 (52 days)
SEC. 1402 contains four subsections (a)-(d), each extracted as a separate provision amending different subsections of the same Section 119
Both sections appropriate 'such sums as may be necessary' with no specific dollar amounts; actual outlays depend on pro rata calculations based on FY2019 authorized levels through November 21, 2019
SEC. 1604 establishes $2,387,000,000 in mandatory spending for the Medicaid Improvement Fund in FY2025 while zeroing out the existing $1,000,000 balance; commonly used as a budget offset mechanism
SEC. 1602 triples WTC Health Program enrollment caps without altering annual funding limitations per subsection (c)
SEC. 1701 contains only budget enforcement exemptions for Division B; no spending provisions in Title VII
